review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with Kubernetes security controls related to configuration change management. However, a slight modification was made to improve clarity in the RBAC function name. The use of admission controllers, RBAC roles, and audit logging are appropriate mechanisms for enforcing configuration change control in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'configuration_change' instead of 'change_control' for consistency with terminology
title: CCCS CM-3: Configuration Change Control
step1_reasoning: Configuration change control in Kubernetes can be enforced through admission controllers that validate or reject changes based on predefined policies. RBAC can define roles that specifically manage configuration changes, ensuring only authorized users can make changes. Additionally, Kubernetes audit logs can be configured to log configuration changes, providing a trail of what changes were made and by whom.
